allow:
Bash(git commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
feat: Fix all parser sheet name detection and improve data extraction

Major improvements to parser reliability and data extraction:

Parser Enhancements:
- KSCP: Add support for ''월종합'', ''보고용자료'', ''사업계획'' sheets
- KCTR: Add ''★PL(Report)'' support with cumulative format parsing, English account names
- KSCE: Add ''PL(KR)'', ''PL(Report)'', ''PL2026(KR)'' sheet detection
- All parsers: Flexible sheet name matching for different file formats

Engine Improvements:
- Fix output path handling for cross-platform compatibility
- Normalize Windows paths when running on Linux/WSL
- Improve settings.json path resolution

Results:
- Total extracted: 748 records across 5 entities
- KSCP: 360 records (252 actual + 108 plan)
- KCTR: 75 records (9 actual + 66 plan)
- KSCCZ: 125 records (29 actual + 96 plan)
- KSCE: 96 records (40 actual + 56 plan)
- KSCI: 92 records (20 actual + 72 plan)

Documentation:
- Update README with comprehensive file format documentation
- Add supported sheet names for each entity
- Add version history and changelog

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")

Bash(git commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
feat: Create v1.2 release with updated project names

Version Management (Following Project Policy):
- Create new KSC_Refiner_v1.2 folder from v1.1
- Preserve v1.1 folder for version history

Project Name Updates:
- ksc_launcher: AssemblyName → KscRefiner_v1.2
- ksc_setup: AssemblyName → KscRefiner_Setup_v1.2
- All projects: Version → 1.2.0

Configuration:
- Add .gitignore for build outputs
- Engine version already set to v1.2
- Inno Setup version set to 1.2.0

Testing:
- ✅ Python engine test passed (748 records)
- ✅ All parsers working correctly
- ✅ Version display: "KSC Settlement Refiner Engine v1.2"

Ready for Windows Build:
- Run build.bat on Windows to create:
  - ksc_engine.exe (Python engine)
  - KscRefiner_v1.2.exe (C# launcher)
  - KscRefiner_Setup_v1.2.0.exe (installer)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")
